english: v. to shout or say something loudly
example: "How will I ever get down?" cried Jimmy.

english: n. something said or done to cause laughter
example: The bearded man laughed as if it was a joke.
If I tell you my ideas, I will lose the chess game.
I'll be dragged down. I don't want that.
"Only after we finish playing the chess game," replied the bearded man.

english: v. to move something back and forth or up and down
example: The beared man replied while shaking his head.

english: v. to have something with others
example: We go down when we are ready to share our ideas with people.

english: v. to stop someone from doing or saying something
v. to look toward someone or something
example: Then the old men turned to face each other.
